source_rationale: Notably, B cells, T cells (CD4/CD8), and natural killer (NK) cells are positioned in adjacent regions, reflecting their shared developmental origin, whereas monocytes (Mono) and granulocyte-macrophage progenitors (GMP) are located further apart, consistent with known hematopoietic differentiation trajectories.
